Strict Browser QA · EVAL lane (Magpie)

Agent-Recover — QA Verdict

Phone-width, through the live companion UI (CDP real gestures). Matrix R1–R5 + touch target. 2026-07-24.

✅ R2 PASS — commit hold LIFTED Post-fix 9c27048, independent UI re-verification against the exact-context gate: claude resumes its exact session + original first-turn, codex resumes its exact rollout 1:1 (no stray/cross-wire), and an unbound agent honestly fails (position-only + cannot-restore toast, no stray). Measured POST-BOOT — not the pre-boot id compare that trapped the first pass.

Re-verification POST-FIX 9c27048 · 2026-07-24

R2

Exact-context restore — measured POST-BOOT through the UI

Pass
method: capture pre-kill original → recover → boot → read the node's LIVE ref + first-turn (never a pre-boot id compare)
harnesspre-kill originalpost-boot LIVE reffirst-turn
claude mrxuxegb3465da3b361eda3b361etrace T1 = "PROBE-CLAUDE-…OK" (original)✅ exact
codex mrxuxrx25248rollout…019f9048rollout…019f9048ref used by 1 node — no cross-wire✅ exact 1:1
unbound mrxuy46c2270(no ref)(NONE)position-only, no stray grabbed✅ honest-fail

The unbound case is the crucial one — it returns a warn toast and binds nothing, instead of the old silent mixup:

warn → Recovered Probe unbound mrxuy46c2270's position, but its exact session couldn't be located — not resumed
R3

Deferred + boot-on-activate — exact context

Pass
recover a snapshot-backed probe while its workspace is inactive → deferred → activate → boot

Recovering mrxuuf7z8010 from an inactive Cookrew Dev → deferred toast; row stayed inactive (node present, not booted). Activating Cookrew Dev → the node booted, and its post-boot live ref is ee42a8c0 — the exact snapshot session.

defer → Recovered Probe claude mrxuuf7z8010 into Cookrew Dev — resumes when that workspace opens
▸ Original failure (pre-fix) — what R2 looked like when it FAILED
R2

One-tap restore position + session

Fail
found by the user · confirmed on-disk: the recovered node runs a session that isn't the snapshot's

The live "recovered" Playground Claude Code restored its position and name, but its claude session diverged — it boots a different session id with 0 checkpoints instead of resuming the snapshot's session. The original greetings conversation still exists on disk, orphaned.

session idfirst turncheckpoints
Original (snapshot)fbb45eb6"hello world" / "what's your name"36 lines — intact on disk
Live "recovered" node cc5f81e9f399ca90"where are you" (unrelated)0

Not isolated: the Recover Probe Claude that this eval initially passed has snapshot session 7adc6b7f — which has no session file on disk at all, so its "resume" is equally hollow. The earlier pass only confirmed the node carried the id before boot, never that the booted agent resumes with history.

Second failure mode — Codex restores the WRONG session (context mixed up)

A recovered Playground Codex came back wired to a different, older rollout from hours ago — not the session the user created. Root cause: the snapshot captured no session ref, so recover grabbed a stray rollout; and refs are shared across agents.

node idcodexSessionRefnote
Snapshot (what user created)335ac3bc(NONE)ref never captured
Live "recovered" node5bdc735drollout…23-44-48Jul 23, ~2h old — unrelated session
Probe Codex + Lanternf86f7666 / c1cc89b4rollout…20-46-41 (same!)two agents, one rollout — cross-wired
The gate the user demands — EXACT-CONTEXT RESTORE: a recovered agent must resume the exact session it had when created — same session ref and same first-turn content — not fresh (claude) and not a different/older/shared session (codex). PASS only when the recovered node's live session ref + first turn match the pre-kill original, measured post-boot through the UI.
Correction: this eval first reported R2 PASS from a matching session-id-in-the-node before boot. That was wrong — the user caught both failures. The correct assertion is exact-context restore as defined above (now met — see Re-verification).
R1

Recover shows only on eligible rows

Pass
127 rows across 16 workspaces enumerated

RECOVER renders iff the row is inactive (recoverEligible = active===false) — 100% correlation, absent on every active row. Legacy snapshot-less rows also get one-tap (per the ruling).

R3

Inactive-workspace deferred boot

Partial
deferred toast + node present + boots on activate — but the boot is hollow (see R2)

Recovering a probe while its workspace was inactive → deferred toast + node CREATED (present) + no PTY; switching to that workspace flipped the row INACTIVE→active = PTY booted on activate. The mechanics work; the restored session is empty, same as R2.

R4

Double-tap idempotent

Pass

Rapid double-tap on a RECOVER button → exactly one node created (f86f7666), not two.

R5

Toast copy vs contract

Pass · 3/3 verbatim

All three toast variants match recover.ts exactly:

ok   → Recovered Recover Probe Codex defer → Recovered Recover Probe Claude into Cookrew Dev — resumes when that workspace opens warn → Recovered Lantern (best-effort — pre-snapshot kill, session may start fresh)

Note the bitter irony: the legacy copy warns "session may start fresh" — and that's precisely what happens for snapshot-backed agents too, which is the R2 bug. Touch target: .roster-recover::after inset:-12px -6px → ~47px hit-area over a 23px button (phone-44px ✓).

Root cause & routing → Forge (backend)

  • Root cause — claude: recover mints / diverges a new claudeSessionId at boot instead of resuming the snapshot's stored session (never passes claude --resume <snapshotSessionId>). Result: claude starts fresh; original transcript orphaned.
  • Root cause — codex: the snapshot captures no codexSessionRef for some agents (Playground Codex = NONE), so recover binds a stray/older rollout; and one rollout ref is shared across multiple agents (Probe Codex + Lantern) — session cross-wiring.
  • Fix: capture each agent's exact session ref at kill-snapshot time (claude id + codex rollout path), keep them 1:1 per agent, and boot recover on that exact ref (true resume). Never fall back to "most recent rollout".
  • QA gate to add (the user's ask): assert exact-context restore — recovered node's live session ref + first-turn content == the pre-kill original, per agent, post-boot through the UI. Never a matching id before boot; never a shared/stray ref.
matrix: agent-recover R1–R5 · verified through the live mobile companion (CDP, 390px) · session evidence from ~/.claude/projects + ~/.cookrew/recoverable.json
First pass: R2 FAIL (fresh/wrong session). Post-fix 9c27048 re-verification: R1–R5 ALL PASS · R2 exact-context confirmed POST-BOOT · commit hold LIFTED · evaluated by Magpie · EVAL lane · 2026-07-24
